title: SAAS-bolagen med potential: ”Generellt överdriven oro”
summary_md:
# SAAS-bolagen med potential: ”Generellt överdriven oro”

**Feed**: dagen-med-di  
**Published**: 2026-06-09  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

The episode examines Nordic SaaS companies, arguing that the sharp valuation declines reflect overdone sector fear driven by AI disruption worries and outflows from small-cap funds, even as fundamentals show genuinely weaker demand and slower growth (medians of 4-11% versus 20%+ in 2021-2022). The hosts believe larger, profitable, cash-generative names like Vitec and Smartcraft will lead any normalization, while select smaller players with strong growth and improving cost discipline offer attractive cases at low multiples. Very few companies meet the Rule of 40, and over half are unprofitable, so selectivity is key.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 VIT-B — Vitec | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Profitable, strong cash flows; positioned to lead SaaS sector normalization after overdone valuation declines.

#### Reasoning

Hosts highlight Vitec as one of the larger SaaS names with strong cash flows and profitability that will likely lead the sector's recovery/normalization seen in Q1. Sector fear (AI threat, small-cap fund outflows) is viewed as overdone relative to fundamentals.

#### Sell Triggers

- if demand/growth deteriorates further
- if Q-on-Q normalization fails to materialize

> *"Vi har ju Vitec och Smartcraft i den här grafen, och de har ju starka kassaflöden. De är lönsamma, fina bolag. Så de kommer ju förmodligen leda den här återhämtningen"*

---

### 🟢 SMCRT — Smartcraft | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Profitable, strong cash flows; positioned alongside Vitec to lead SaaS sector recovery.

#### Reasoning

Cited together with Vitec as a larger, profitable, cash-generative SaaS company expected to drive the sector's normalization/rebound. Hosts see general sector worry as overdone relative to fundamentals.

#### Sell Triggers

- if demand weakens further
- if profitability or cash flows deteriorate

> *"Vi har ju Vitec och Smartcraft i den här grafen, och de har ju starka kassaflöden. De är lönsamma, fina bolag."*

---

### 🟢 BRIX — Briox | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Strong growth off a low Lithuanian base, driven by a successful AI accounting-service platform.

#### Reasoning

Hosts single out Briox as standing out on growth, noting it comes from a low base in Lithuania, which amplifies growth rates, but crucially has succeeded with its AI-driven accounting-service platform, delivering strong growth.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI accounting platform adoption stalls
- if growth normalizes as base effect fades

> *"Det är framförallt Briox... de har lyckats väldigt väl med sin AI-ekonomistjänst som har en plattform för det. De har haft en väldigt fin tillväxt."*

---

### 🟢 FREJA — Freja eID Group | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Grown strongly but shares down 7% YTD with little fundamental justification; oversold on sector fear.

#### Reasoning

Hosts note Freja has grown strongly yet the stock has traded down 7% this year despite no fundamentals supporting the decline, implying it was dragged down by broad sector worry — a potential mispricing/recovery candidate at low multiples.

#### Sell Triggers

- if growth slows
- if demand weakens as in broader SaaS sector

> *"Freja i det har också växt ganska kraftigt. Men trots att de har växt kraftigt så har aktien handlats ner 7% i år."*

---

## Summary Table

|--------|---------|--------|----|------|---------|
| **VIT-B** | Vitec | 🟢 BUY | MT | 70% | Profitable, strong cash flows; positioned to lead SaaS sector normalization after overdone valuation declines. |

| **BRIX** | Briox | 🟢 BUY | MT | 60% | Strong growth off a low Lithuanian base, driven by a successful AI accounting-service platform. |
